user_query:
I own Smoky River BBQ in Kansas City. Please use the attached local files to prioritize and implement quick on-page SEO improvements for our Kansas City pages, and deliver both updated files and a concise owner-friendly update.

Inputs:
- input/pages.csv — last month’s search metrics per page
- input/site.yaml — brand and local context
- input/pages/*.html — current page HTML files

Tasks and constraints:
1) Filter and rank pages for local SEO opportunity:
   - Filter to rows where city == "Kansas City", impressions >= 100, ctr < 0.10, and 3 <= avg_position <= 15.
   - Compute opportunity_score = impressions * (0.10 - ctr) using ctr as a decimal from the CSV (e.g., 0.06).
   - Sort remaining pages by opportunity_score in descending order.
   - Save the full sorted results to output/ranked_pages.csv with these columns: file_name, url_path, city, impressions, ctr, avg_position, primary_keyword, opportunity_score.

2) Rewrite the HTML <title> and <meta name="description"> for the top 5 ranked pages only (do not modify any others):
   - Use brand and context from input/site.yaml (brand: Smoky River BBQ; city: Kansas City; target_terms list) to guide wording.
   - New <title> requirements: must be 60 characters or fewer; must include the page’s exact primary_keyword from pages.csv and the brand name "Smoky River BBQ".
   - New <meta name="description"> requirements: length between 120 and 160 characters inclusive; must include "Kansas City" and one term from site.yaml target_terms (BBQ, barbecue, or smoked meats); and must end with one call-to-action phrase chosen from: "Order now", "Book today", "Call us", or "Learn more".
   - Preserve all other HTML content and structure; only update the <title> and the existing <meta name="description"> tag.
   - Save edited copies to output/optimized_pages/ using the same file names as in input/pages/.

3) Compose a short owner update at output/owner_update.md:
   - 4–6 sentences in plain English summarizing what you did, why these pages were selected, and expected impact.
   - Include a bulleted list for each modified page with: file_name, computed opportunity_score, original vs. new <title> character counts, and original vs. new meta description character counts.
   - End with a brief "Next steps" section with 3 action bullets (e.g., internal links, schema, image alt updates).

Deliverables:
- output/ranked_pages.csv (ranked with computed opportunity_score, sorted descending)
- output/optimized_pages/<original-file-names>.html (only the top 5 pages, titles and meta descriptions updated per rules)
- output/owner_update.md (status-style summary with bullets and next steps)

Use only the provided local files; do not fetch anything online. All three parts are required for completion. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
